mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly higher during periods of GDP growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals weakening labor markets, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive despite rising unemployment (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates strong demand and accommodative policy, boosting S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS does not result in positive returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during a tightening cycle signal higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive during tightening periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production indicates robust supply-side growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly higher during INDPRO growth (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive despite falling HOUST (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicate a healthy labor market, boosting S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE does not result in positive returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation signals central bank intervention, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive despite high inflation (p > 0.05).
